meta.status: draft — validate with 3-5 agency contacts before publishing
meta.rationale: DFR buyers procure like defense (program-based, compliance-gated, grant-funded) but with smaller budgets and less urgency than DoD. 75% reflects complexity without pricing out municipal buyers.

free_tier.name: DFR Intel Free
free_tier.purpose: Moat — pull agency staff into the ecosystem before paywall
includes: Platform specs for all NDAA-compliant DFR platforms (Skydio, BRINC, Flock Alpha, Parrot ANAFI USA, Inspired Flight IF800/IF1200A)
NDAA / Blue UAS compliance status per platform
DHS AEL 03OE-07-SUAS eligibility status per platform
BEYOND waiver approval count by state (aggregate, no agency PII)
Active DFR program count by state (EFF Atlas data, updated monthly)
FAA Part 108 status tracker (live monitoring)
DFR vendor landscape overview (Skydio, BRINC, Flock, Flying Lion, SkyfireAI)
free_tier.access: No registration required — public Forge dashboard
free_tier.update_cadence: Weekly for compliance data, monthly for program counts

tiers.name: DFR Intel Basic
tiers.target_buyer: Agency procurement officer or grant administrator doing initial research
tiers.best_for: Agencies in early evaluation phase — 'can we afford this, can we get a grant for it'
includes: Grant Eligibility Report — platform x grant program matrix for up to 3 platforms
Platform recommendation for 1 specified mission type
NDAA/ASDA/AEL compliance documentation checklist (per platform)
State SAA contact information and application cycle for agency's state
Budget estimate template (single-site)
tiers.example_use_case: Springfield IL PD wants to know if they can buy a Skydio X10D with COPS Tech grant and what documentation they need

tiers.name: DFR Intel Full
tiers.target_buyer: Program manager or chief preparing to stand up DFR program
tiers.best_for: Agencies that have decided to move forward and need the full roadmap
includes: Everything in Basic
FAA BEYOND Authorization Pathway Guide (jurisdiction-specific where data available)
Full CAD integration matrix for selected platforms
Vendor support ratings for all DFR vendors
Program standup checklist (all 7 phases)
Mission type → platform recommendation for up to 3 mission types
Dock/infrastructure recommendation
Ohio DFR Pilot Program template analysis (if state applicable)
Budget estimate template (multi-site)
DFR service provider comparison (Flying Lion vs SkyfireAI vs SkyFire Consulting)
tiers.delivery: PDF report + JSON data download + editable checklist
tiers.example_use_case: Cook County Sheriff IL planning a 3-dock county-wide DFR program and needs the full procurement and authorization roadmap

tiers.name: DFR State Program Intelligence
tiers.target_buyer: State homeland security office, state DOT, or consulting firm managing state DFR program
tiers.best_for: States considering or launching statewide DFR programs (Ohio model replication)
includes: Everything in Full Intel
Statewide DFR program landscape (all active programs in state + neighboring states)
ODOT/Ohio pilot program full structure analysis and replication guide
Vendor pre-approval list benchmarking (what other states approve)
State SAA HSGP/SHSP allocation data and DFR investment justification examples
Legislative tracking (drone program bills in target state)
Competitive vendor analysis for state program RFP development
tiers.delivery: PDF + JSON + editable program design document
tiers.example_use_case: Texas DPS considering statewide DFR pilot program after Ohio model

tiers.name: DFR Database API Access
tiers.target_buyer: Equipment manufacturers, insurance firms, grant consultants, drone integrators
tiers.best_for: Organizations that want to query the DFR intelligence database directly
includes: Read-only API access to DFR-tagged canonical database
Platform compliance records (NDAA, Blue UAS, AEL status)
Grant program data (active cycles, award amounts, eligibility)
Vendor landscape data
CAD integration matrix
Weekly data refresh
tiers.notes: Future revenue stream as database matures. Prioritize report tiers first.

pricing_rationale.vs_defense_vertical: 75% of defense rate. Defense Full Intel equivalent is ~$600 → DFR Full Intel at $449. Defense subscription equivalent at $400/mo → DFR at $299/mo.
pricing_rationale.vs_market: No direct neutral competitor exists. Skydio, BRINC, Flock all provide vendor-specific guidance — not neutral. Flying Lion and SkyfireAI charge $20-50K for full program standup consulting. DroneClear reports offer a self-service intelligence layer at 1-10% of full consulting cost.
pricing_rationale.free_tier_moat: Free tier pulls in agency staff who are researching — they become familiar with Forge before the paywall. Platform specs and compliance status are commodity data; the reports are the product.
pricing_rationale.grant_alignment: At $149-449 per report, agencies can expense this against grant administration budgets. Grant administrators routinely spend this on research tools.

go_to_market.beta_pricing: Free for first 5 validated agency contacts — in exchange for feedback on report templates
go_to_market.soft_launch_milestone: 3 positive beta responses + report templates validated → open paid tier
channels: DRONERESPONDERS community (free membership — engage, share DFR Intel Free content)
ALEA / IACP conference presence (conference season Q3/Q4)
Direct outreach to active DFR program managers via LinkedIn
National League of Cities — monitor city contacts adopting DFR via NLC/BRINC program
